---
title: Rain Dance
theme: "cerulean"
highlight-style: github
author: github.com/temataro
format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
---
<!-- # FIGURE TEMPLATE -->
<!-- # ```{python} -->
<!-- # #| label:       FIGURE_LABEL -->
<!-- # #| fig-cap:     FIGURE_CAPTION -->
<!-- # #| fig-subcap: -->
<!-- # #|    - "SUBFIG CAP #1" -->
<!-- # #|    - "SUBFIG CAP #2" -->
<!-- # #| layout-nrow: -->
<!-- # #| -->
<!-- # #| layout-ncol: -->
<!-- #  -->
<!-- # fig = plt.figure() -->
<!-- # plt.plot(YOU_KNOW_WHAT_GOES_HERE, "-") -->
<!-- # plt.xlim((-1., 1.)) -->
<!-- # plt.ylim((-2., 2.)) -->
<!-- # plt.xlabel("") -->
<!-- # plt.ylabel("") -->
<!-- # plt.title("Plot title here.") -->
<!-- # plt.grid(True) -->
<!-- # ``` -->

# The News Website Who Cried Rain
The problem is that without fail, I will fall for each and every article that
tells me rain is expected today.

![](./media/ted-mosby.jpg)

I have been here 6 years and I've filled in more mm of precipitation from my
tears.

Let's actually call them out on this:
An un-named gulf news source vs the NOAA Climate Data Online historic data
summary from Jan. 1, 2020 until Aug. 24, 2025.

```{python}
import polars as pl
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

from datetime import datetime

DATA_SRC = "../dataset/4186532.csv"

"""
output of `head ../dataset/4186532.csv`
"STATION","NAME","DATE","PRCP"
"AEM00041217","ABU DHABI INTERNATIONAL, AE","2020-01-01",
"AEM00041217","ABU DHABI INTERNATIONAL, AE","2020-01-02",
"AEM00041217","ABU DHABI INTERNATIONAL, AE","2020-01-03",
"AEM00041217","ABU DHABI INTERNATIONAL, AE","2020-01-04",
"AEM00041217","ABU DHABI INTERNATIONAL, AE","2020-01-05",
"AEM00041217","ABU DHABI INTERNATIONAL, AE","2020-01-06",
"AEM00041217","ABU DHABI INTERNATIONAL, AE","2020-01-07",
"AEM00041217","ABU DHABI INTERNATIONAL, AE","2020-01-08",
"AEM00041217","ABU DHABI INTERNATIONAL, AE","2020-01-09",
"""
DATA_DTYPE = {
  "STATION": pl.Utf8,
  "NAME": pl.Utf8,
  "DATE": pl.Datetime,
  "PRCP": pl.Float32
}
```

Let's read this into a dataframe and plot all the times it's rained in the last
6 years.

```{python}
plt.style.use("../computermodern.mplstyle")

df = pl.read_csv(
  DATA_SRC, separator=',', has_header=True, schema_overrides=DATA_DTYPE
)
# Fill in the empty prcp columns with zeros
df = df.with_columns(
  pl.col("PRCP").fill_null(0)
)
```
```{python}
#| label:       Precipitation Over Jan. 1, 2020 - Aug. 24, 2025
#| fig-cap:     Precipitation Over Jan. 1, 2020 - Aug. 24, 2025

fig = plt.figure(dpi=180)
plt.plot(df["DATE"], df["PRCP"])
plt.title("Precip. over 6-ish years")
rain_day_2024 = df.filter(
  pl.col("DATE") == datetime(2024, 4, 16)
).head(1)
plt.axvspan(xmin=datetime(2024,1,1), xmax=datetime(2024, 5, 1), color="#6400ff", alpha=0.3, label="Most rain ever,\n Spring 2024")
plt.annotate(
  text="That day it rained so hard\neverything blew away in the wind.\nApr. 16, 2024",
  xy=(rain_day_2024["DATE"][0], rain_day_2024["PRCP"][0]),
  xytext=(datetime(2022, 1, 1), 40),
  arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle="->")
)
plt.xlabel("Date")
plt.ylabel("Precipitation (mm)")
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True)
```

### SO, according to NASA, how many times has it rained over the last 6-ish years?
```{python}
rained_days = df.filter(
  pl.col("PRCP") > 0.0
)

pl.Config.set_tbl_rows(30)
pl.Config.set_tbl_cols(20)
pl.Config.set_tbl_width_chars(140)
pl.Config.set_tbl_formatting("ASCII_MARKDOWN")
pl.Config.set_fmt_str_lengths(80)
pl.Config.set_float_precision(3)

print(rained_days.select(["NAME", "DATE", "PRCP"]))
print(f"\n\nOver the period of time between "
      f"{df['DATE'].head(1)[0]} and "
      f"{df['DATE'].tail(1)[0]}..."
      f"({len(df['DATE']):,} days),"
      "\nIt has rained a total of "
      f"{rained_days.shape[0]} times."
)
```

## The problem

According to news websites whose names I won't mention *ahem* *ahem* rhymes
with _shmulf bews_ *ahem* *ahem*, I should expect rain almost once a week!
I should be expecting it in the middle of Abu Dhabi July and packing a jacket
just in case! I should be cancelling outdoor dates and carrying my umbrella
everywhere I go... yeah sure.


SO, let's learn how to (hopefully ethically) scrape their news articles for
mentions of rain over the current decade and come up with a percentage
likelihood for how much rain we're expected to see according to each article
that mentions rain.

The sitemap for gulfnews.com looks like this:
`https://gulfnews.com/sitemap/sitemap-daily-2026-02-21.xml`

Each day, an entry.
I just need to extract the contents of each day's headlines and filter for
keywords like 'rain', 'rainfall', 'precipitation', etc. and then save articles
that match into a jsonl file sorted by month.

After python-ing with gpt5 on opencode using xmls and beautifulsoup4, I get something that looks like
this:
```
{"url": "https://gulfnews.com/world/asia/india/india-pm-narendra-modi-to-contest-from-varanasi-as-bjp-kickstarts-poll-campaign-1.1709408251331", "lastmod": "2024-03-02", "title": "India PM Narendra Modi to contest from Varanasi as BJP kickstarts poll campaign", "title_matches_keywords": false, "text": null}
{"url": "https://gulfnews.com/world/mena/us-military-begins-air-dropping-aid-to-gaza-1.1709404935406", "lastmod": "2024-03-02", "title": "US military begins air-dropping aid to Gaza", "title_matches_keywords": false, "text": null}
{"url": "https://gulfnews.com/business/wtos-13th-ministerial-conference-a-major-milestone-to-develop-the-global-trade-system-says-chairman-of-added-1.1709401457798", "lastmod": "2024-03-02", "title": "WTO\u2019s 13th Ministerial Conference a major milestone to develop the global trade system, says chairman of ADDED", "title_matches_keywords": false, "text": null}
{"url": "https://gulfnews.com/photos/news/pictures-inaugural-edition-of-gulf-news-edufair-schools--nurseries-kicks-off-in-dubai-1.1709397569879", "lastmod": "2024-03-03", "title": "Pictures: Inaugural edition of Gulf News Edufair Schools & Nurseries kicks off in Dubai", "title_matches_keywords": false, "text": null}
{"url":
"https://gulfnews.com/uae/weather/uae-weather-update-rain-thunder-and-lightning-expected-to-hit-the-country-starting-monday-1.1709394185115",
"lastmod": "2024-03-03", "title": "UAE weather update: Rain, thunder and
lightning expected to hit the country starting Monday",
"title_matches_keywords": true, "text": "Abu Dhabi: The National Center of
Meteorology (NCM) has stated that the UAE is being affected by a surface
low-pressure system extending from the Southwest, accompanied by a cold air
mass from the West in the upper atmosphere, resulting in a flow of clouds..On
Monday and Tuesday, cloud cover is expected to gradually increase from the
afternoon into the evening from the West, extending across scattered areas of
the country. This will be accompanied by convective clouds, associated with
rainfall of varying intensities, and at times heavy with lightning and thunder
in some areas..Click here to get exclusive content with Gulf News WhatsApp
channel.By Wednesday, the amount of clouds and rainfall is forecast to
decrease. However, the weather is expected to remain partly cloudy to cloudy at
times, particularly in southern regions, with a decrease in temperatures..The
sea conditions are expected to be moderate to rough, especially with the
presence of convective clouds in the Arabian Gulf and the Oman Sea."}
```

For each url found in the day's sitemap, we skim the title to see if anything
relates to weather. If it does, go to the url and extract the article body to
form this jsonl file. The format looks like this:


The main function in charge of doing this is `def extract_sitemap_urls_to_jsonl()` in `../main.py`
```{python}
#| code-fold: true
#| eval: false

import sys
from pathlib import Path

sys.path.append(str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent))

from main import *
def extract_sitemap_urls_to_jsonl(
    start_date,
    end_date,
    output_root=OUTPUT_ROOT,
    request_delay_s=0.5,
    force=False,
):
    output_path = None
    header = (
        f"{ANSI_INFO}╔══════════════════════════════════════════════╗{ANSI_RESET}\n"
        f"{ANSI_INFO}║{ANSI_RESET} {ANSI_BOLD}Gulf News Extraction Console{ANSI_RESET}           {ANSI_INFO}║{ANSI_RESET}\n"
        f"{ANSI_INFO}╚══════════════════════════════════════════════╝{ANSI_RESET}"
    )
    print(header, flush=True)

    # We'll generate a sitemap url based on the format of the website:
    #    "https://gulfnews.com/sitemap/sitemap-daily-2026-02-21.xml"
    prefix = "https://gulfnews.com/sitemap/sitemap-daily"

    current_date = parse_date(start_date)
    end = parse_date(end_date)

    while current_date < end:
        sitemap_url = f"{prefix}-{current_date.isoformat()}.xml"
        sitemap_date = current_date.isoformat()
        output_path = output_path_for_sitemap(sitemap_date, output_root=output_root)
        # This prevents us from re-bothering the server for data we've already
        # collected, unless the force option is passed.
        if output_path.exists() and not force:
            return output_path

        xml_text = fetch_text(sitemap_url)
        entries = parse_sitemap(xml_text)
        total = len(entries)
        counter = 0
        print(
            f"{ANSI_INFO}[INFO]{ANSI_RESET} "
            f"Date: {ANSI_BOLD}{sitemap_date}{ANSI_RESET} | "
            f"Entries: {ANSI_BOLD}{total}{ANSI_RESET}",
            flush=True,
        )

        # Make a different JSONL for each month of entries that've been filtered
        # through.
        output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

        matches = 0
        with output_path.open("w", encoding="utf-8") as handle:
            for entry in entries:
                counter += 1
                progress = f"{counter}/{total}" if total else "0/0"
                print(
                    f"\r{ANSI_FETCH}[FETCH]{ANSI_RESET} "
                    f"{ANSI_BOLD}{progress}{ANSI_RESET} "
                    f"{ANSI_DIM}{current_date}{ANSI_RESET}",
                    end="",
                    flush=True,
                )
                html_text = fetch_text(entry.url)
                soup = BeautifulSoup(html_text, "html.parser")
                title = extract_title(soup)
                if title == "":
                    print(f"\n{ANSI_WARN}[WARN]{ANSI_RESET} Empty title", flush=True)
                matched = title_matches_keywords(title)
                if matched:
                    matches += 1
                    article_text = extract_article_text(soup)
                else:
                    article_text = None
                handle.write(
                    json.dumps(
                        {
                            "url": entry.url,
                            "lastmod": (
                                entry.lastmod.isoformat() if entry.lastmod else None
                            ),
                            "title": title,
                            "title_matches_keywords": matched,
                            "text": article_text,
                        }
                    )
                    + "\n"
                )
                if request_delay_s:
                    sleep(request_delay_s)
            print(
                f"\r{ANSI_DONE}[DONE]{ANSI_RESET} Day complete with #{matches=}",
                flush=True,
            )

        current_date += timedelta(days=1)

    return output_path
```
